questions.question: What is a discrete random variable?
options: A) A variable that can take on any value
B) A variable that can only take on a finite number of values
C) A variable that can only take on specific values
D) A variable that can only take on non-integer values
questions.explanation: A discrete random variable is a variable that can only take on a finite number of distinct values.

questions.question: What is the expected value of a discrete random variable?
options: A) The sum of the product of each value and its probability
B) The sum of all possible values
C) The average of all possible values
D) The probability of each value
questions.explanation: The expected value of a discrete random variable is the sum of the product of each value and its probability.

questions.question: What is a continuous random variable?
options: A) A variable that can only take on a finite number of values
B) A variable that can take on any value within a certain range
C) A variable that can only take on specific values
D) A variable that can only take on non-integer values
questions.explanation: A continuous random variable is a variable that can take on any value within a certain range.

questions.question: What is the probability density function (PDF) of a continuous random variable?
options: A) A function that describes the probability of each value
B) A function that describes the expected value of each value
C) A function that describes the range of possible values
D) A function that describes the probability of each range of values
questions.explanation: The probability density function (PDF) of a continuous random variable is a function that describes the probability of each value.

questions.question: What is the difference between a discrete random variable and a continuous random variable?
options: A) A discrete random variable can only take on integer values, while a continuous random variable can take on any value
B) A discrete random variable can take on any value, while a continuous random variable can only take on integer values
C) A discrete random variable can only take on specific values, while a continuous random variable can only take on non-integer values
D) A discrete random variable can take on any value within a certain range, while a continuous random variable can take on any value
questions.explanation: The main difference between a discrete random variable and a continuous random variable is that a discrete random variable can only take on a finite number of distinct values, while a continuous random variable can take on any value within a certain range.
